title: Analytics - Device Connected
scenario: Analytics event "device_connected" is logged with device-specific data (PID, Serial) upon successful device connection in Poly Studio Mobile app
steps: 1. Launch the Poly Studio Mobile app on an Android device.
2. On the Home screen, ensure no device is currently connected.
3. Power on a Poly Bluetooth audio device (e.g., Poly Voyager 5200, Poly Savi 7300, etc.).
4. Press the Bluetooth button on the device to make it discoverable.
5. In the Poly Studio Mobile app, tap the device name when it appears on the Home screen to initiate pairing.
6. Wait for the connection to complete (device status shows "Connected").
7. Open the Firebase Analytics dashboard for the Poly Studio Mobile app.
8. Navigate to the Events section and locate the "device_connected" event.
9. Verify that the event appears with default elements: graph, event count by country, event count by gender, events per session, app version, build number, and environment.
10. Verify that the event includes device-specific parameters: Device PID and Device Serial.
11. Note the current event count for "device_connected".
12. Wait a few hours (as specified by the dashboard update latency).
13. Refresh the Firebase dashboard and check the updated event count for "device_connected".
expected: The "device_connected" analytics event is logged in Firebase with default elements (graph, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version/build/environment) and device-specific data (Device PID, Device Serial). Event counts update after a few hours.
